finding: A. Primary CNS tumor: medulloblastoma, glioblastoma, pineal tumors

differentials.name: Secondary tumor: carcinoma of breast + lung, melanoma, NHL, leukemia
differentials.raw: Secondary tumor: carcinoma of breast + lung, melanoma, NHL, leukemia

differentials.name: cranial nerve deficits (40%): visual disturbances, diplopia, hearing loss, facial numbness
differentials.raw: cranial nerve deficits (40%): visual disturbances, diplopia, hearing loss, facial numbness

differentials.name: progressive asymmetric weakness of extremities Dx:     harvest of malignant cells ← lumbar puncture positive in 45-55%
differentials.raw: progressive asymmetric weakness of extremities Dx:     harvest of malignant cells ← lumbar puncture positive in 45-55% (1st puncture), 80% (after several taps)

differentials.name: Dural Meningeal Carcinomatosis »  rarely associated with positive cytology
differentials.raw: Dural Meningeal Carcinomatosis »  rarely associated with positive cytology

differentials.name: Leptomeningeal Carcinomatosis »  frequently associated with positive cytology
differentials.raw: Leptomeningeal Carcinomatosis »  frequently associated with positive cytology

context: Histo:  adenocarcinoma (75%) Spread: (a)  hematogenous dissemination (most common) (b)  contiguous perineural spread (c)  lymphatic spread (d)  shedding of tumor cells from parenchymal metastases Associated with:   concurrent brain parenchymal metastases (in 20%) ◊  Simultaneous occurrence of symptoms localized to more than one area! N.B.:   Perform MRI prior to lumbar puncture ◊  Lumbar puncture contraindicated if intracranial pressure increased! Location:  basal cisterns, lumbar spine (areas of CSF stasis) √  communicating hydrocephalus ← tumor interferes with CSF reabsorption in pacchionian
